positional_bias.notes: Max share 33.3% across positions 0 and 1 — well under 40% fail threshold.

length_bias.notes: q5 correct is longest (237) but delta 33.8 < 20% of mean (40.6). No question flagged.

distractor_plausibility.notes: All distractors represent genuine beginner misconceptions: combined-accuracy confusion, FP/FN direction errors, wrong step ordering, human-baseline misapplication, alert-fatigue inversion.

coverage.objective: Define acceptable error as a deliberate threshold decision
Distinguish FP/FN and explain asymmetric costs
Same error rate acceptable in one context, unacceptable in another
Describe human baseline — useful but insufficient reference point
Alert fatigue as consequence of too-strict thresholds (corpus §6.2)
coverage.note: Not a numbered corpus LO but tested with explicit corpus §6.2 citation in sidecar rationale

beginner_fairness.notes: Stems use plain language throughout. FP/FN spelled out as 'false positive'/'false negative' in stems. All content within topic scope.
